meta_title: For Founders: deployment workflow to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to deployment workflow and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Deployment Workflow to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Stop worrying about your empty portfolio. Learn a high-speed deployment workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch SaaS MVPs that attract clients.
content.answer_block: To overcome a lack of portfolio, shift your focus from 'building code' to 'shipping outcomes.' By implementing an automated deployment workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code, you can turn ideas into live, production-ready SaaS apps in hours, not weeks. This demonstrates technical execution and business velocity to potential investors and paying clients.
content.intro:
The silence of an empty portfolio is deafening when you're pitching for your first high-ticket contract. You have the vision and the technical chops, but the market only trusts what it can see, touch, and test. Staring at a blank repository isn't just a technical hurdle; it’s a revenue-killing bottleneck that keeps you on the sidelines of the SaaS gold rush.

But the era of 'polishing code' is over. Today, the most successful founders are those who master the art of the rapid deployment loop. By leveraging high-leverage tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you can transform a raw concept into a live, scalable application before your competitors have even finished their initial architecture diagrams.

steps.title: Define the MVP Scope for Maximum Impact
steps.description: Don't build a platform; build a wedge. Focus on one specific pain point that proves you can solve a real business problem. We use Antigravity to scaffold the core value proposition.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a clean, production-ready SaaS boilerplate for [Insert Niche] focusing on a single high-conversion user flow. Include Auth, Stripe integration, and a responsive UI layout.

steps.title: Automate the Infrastructure Layer
steps.description: Manual deployment is for amateurs. Use Antigravity to generate infrastructure-as-code scripts that ensure your environment is identical to production from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Terraform configuration for an AWS-based serverless deployment pipeline including CI/CD integration with GitHub Actions.

steps.title: Build the Core Feature with Claude Code
steps.description: Use Claude Code to iterate on your core feature. Focus on 'happy path' logic that you can showcase in a video demo to prove your product works.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a service layer for [Core Feature] that handles data processing and returns an API response ready for a frontend dashboard.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write --task 'implement core business logic for feature X'

steps.title: Implement Continuous Deployment
steps.description: Every push to main should go to a staging environment. This creates a 'live' link you can send to prospects immediately, even if it's not feature-complete.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow file that triggers on push to main and deploys the build to a Vercel staging environment automatically.

steps.title: Polish and Publicize
steps.description: Add a professional landing page to your app. A live URL in your portfolio is worth more than 10,000 lines of unreleased code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a high-converting landing page section using Tailwind CSS that highlights the value proposition and includes a 'Get Early Access' call to action.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write --task 'add landing page component to src/pages/index.tsx'

pro_tips: Always include a 'View Source' link in your footer; it builds extreme trust with technical founders who want to see your code quality.
Use a custom domain for every MVP project; it signals to potential clients that you treat your side projects like real businesses.
Focus your portfolio on 'The Problem Solved' rather than 'The Tech Stack Used'—founders care about revenue, not your framework choice.
Record a 60-second Loom demo of the app in action and embed it on the landing page to bypass the need for users to sign up.
Include a 'Roadmap' page in your app to show you are thinking about product-market fit, not just coding features.
content.next_steps: Once your first app is live, share the link on LinkedIn and Twitter with a breakdown of the problem you solved. Then, rinse and repeat to build three distinct apps in different niches to show versatility.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped with the workflow to bridge the gap between 'no portfolio' and 'proven founder.' Stop waiting for permission to build and start shipping products that generate authority and revenue. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
